response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman (web, mobile, embedded tooling) with strong breadth across TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and some C/C++, but the role demands SoC and IP modeling, AI-accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design at the silicon level. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture, or chip-level performance work. The low-latency DLang and LabWindows/CVI experience is the closest adjacency but remains far from the hardware-architecture core of this Principal Performance Architect position. Education is a BS in CS, which can satisfy the BS+8 path, but the experience domain is fundamentally mismatched.
strengths: Deep, long-tenured software engineering experience across many stacks
Some systems/low-latency and embedded exposure (DLang async I/O, C/C++ for LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, teaching)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator, or GPU-architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD background
Profile is web/full-stack rather than hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-arch experience
Some low-latency and embedded C/C++ exposure
